html:
[5454]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 冠凫 - A mythical bird that is said to transform from a fish during autumn; symbolizes change and transformation.
  • 石首鱼 - A type of fish known for its head containing a stone; literal translation.
  • 秦吉了 - A type of bird, possibly a parrot, known for its ability to mimic human speech; common theme in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 隙中伺之 - To secretly observe from a crack or opening; a common motif in Chinese literature and folklore, often associated with supernatural events.
  • 化为白鸟 - To transform into a white bird; describes a supernatural event.
  • 飞去 - To fly away; describes the departure of a transformed bird.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 石首鱼至秋化为冠凫 - Mythological transformation where a fish becomes a bird during autumn; symbolizes change and transformation.
  • 秦吉了 - A bird known for its ability to mimic human speech; common theme in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 容管廉白州 - A specific region in China, possibly known for its unique flora and fauna.
  • 汧阳郡 - A historical county in China, mentioned in the context of a temple and a supernatural event.
  • 张女郎庙 - A temple dedicated to a female deity or historical figure; indicates the importance of female figures in local religious practices.

Proper Nouns

  • 冠凫 - A mythical bird.
  • 石首鱼 - A type of fish.
  • 秦吉了 - A type of bird.
  • 容管廉白州 - A place name.
  • 汧阳郡 - A county in ancient China.
  • 张女郎庙 - A temple.
  • 韦氏子 - A person named Wei.
  • 郡守 - The governor of a county.
  • 馆亭 - A pavilion or hall.

[5455]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鳥賊 - A colloquial term combining '鳥' (bird) and '賊' (thief), likely referring to someone who is mischievous or troublesome, possibly in the context of hunting or causing disturbance.
  • 鳥省 - A playful term combining '鳥' (bird) and '省' (province or department), likely referring to a place where birds are kept or managed, akin to a 'bird department'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李靖 - A famous general and military strategist during the Tang Dynasty, known for his contributions to the establishment of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 右武卫将军 - A military rank in the Tang Dynasty, indicating a high-ranking officer in the imperial guard.
  • 京师 - Refers to the capital city of Chang'an during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 澧水 - A river in southern China, possibly referring to a specific location near the capital.
  • 則天 - A title for Empress Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history.
  • 左卫兵曹 - A military position in the Tang Dynasty, indicating a lower-ranking officer in the imperial guard.
  • 吉了鸟 - A type of parrot known for its ability to mimic human speech, often kept as a pet or used in entertainment.

Proper Nouns

  • 李靖 - A famous general and military strategist during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 右武卫将军 - A military rank in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 京师 - The capital city of Chang'an during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 澧水 - A river in southern China.
  • 則天 - A title for Empress Wu Zetian.
  • 左卫兵曹 - A military position in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 吉了鸟 - A type of parrot known for its ability to mimic human speech.
  • 劉景陽 - A historical figure, possibly a government official, involved in the incident with the talking bird.

[5456]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 食蝗鸟 - A literal phrase meaning 'bird that eats locusts,' used to describe a specific type of bird known for its diet.
  • 开元中 - Refers to the early years of the Kaiyuan era (713-741) during the Tang Dynasty, a period of prosperity and cultural flourishing.
  • 贝州 - A place name, referring to Beizhou, a region in Hebei Province.
  • 小白鸟数万 - Literally 'thousands of small white birds,' used to describe a large number of birds.
  • 大白鸟数千 - Literally 'thousands of large white birds,' used to describe a large number of larger birds.
  • 尽食其虫 - Means 'ate all the insects,' emphasizing the complete consumption of the locusts.
  • 出酉阳杂爼 - Refers to the source of the information, likely a historical or anecdotal collection.
  • 病中独卧 - Means 'lying alone sick,' describing the state of the person.
  • 俄止庭树 - Means 'soon stopped at the courtyard tree,' describing the bird's action.
  • 目大如柸 - Means 'eyes as big as cups,' using a simile to describe the size of the bird's eyes.
  • 嘴长尺余 - Means 'beak over a foot long,' describing the length of the bird's beak.
  • 下地上阶 - Means 'landed on the ground and stepped on the stairs,' describing the bird's movement.
  • 入房登牀 - Means 'entered the room and climbed onto the bed,' describing the bird's further actions.
  • 举两翅翅有子 - Means 'lifted both wings, each wing having a child,' describing the bird's wings.
  • 持小枪欲以撃融 - Means 'holding a small spear, intending to strike Lu Rong,' describing the bird's action.
  • 伏惧流汗 - Means 'lying down in fear, sweating,' describing the person's reaction.
  • 忽复有人从后门入 - Means 'suddenly someone else entered from the back door,' introducing another character.
  • 此是善人慎勿伤也 - Means 'this is a good person, be careful not to harm them,' providing a warning.
  • 鸟遂飞去 - Means 'the bird then flew away,' describing the bird's departure.
  • 人亦随出 - Means 'the person also left,' describing the person's departure.
  • 疾自尔永差 - Means 'the illness was cured forever,' describing the outcome.
  • 出广异记 - Refers to the source of the information, likely a collection of strange and unusual stories.
  • 濮州刺史李全璋妻张牛肃之姨也 - Means 'Zhang, the wife of Li Quanzhang, the governor of Puzhou, was the aunt of Niu Su,' providing background information.
  • 卒于伊阙庄 - Means 'died at Yiquan Manor,' describing the location of death.
  • 寝疾 - Means 'lying ill,' describing the state of the person.
  • 止于庭树 - Means 'stopped at the courtyard tree,' describing the bird's action.
  • 白首赤足黄腹丹翅 - Means 'white head, red feet, yellow belly, red wings,' describing the bird's appearance.
  • 其鸣但云懊恨也 - Means 'its call was only 'grief and regret,' describing the bird's sound.
  • 母兮如是 - Means 'mother, like this,' a phrase that might be part of the bird's call.
  • 昼夜不絶声 - Means 'unceasing throughout day and night,' describing the frequency of the bird's call.
  • 十余日 - Means 'over ten days,' describing the duration.
  • 鸟遂不见 - Means 'the bird then disappeared,' describing the bird's departure.
  • 出纪闻 - Refers to the source of the information, likely a collection of anecdotes.
  • 天寳末 - Refers to the end of the Tianbao era (755-756) during the Tang Dynasty, a period marked by political turmoil and the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 台州录事叅军王绪病将死 - Means 'Wang Xu, a recorder of the state of Taizhou, was dying,' providing background information.
  • 有大鸟飞入绪房 - Means 'a large bird flew into Wang Xu's room,' describing the bird's action.
  • 行至牀所 - Means 'walked to the bed,' describing the bird's movement.
  • 引嘴向绪 - Means 'extended its beak toward Wang Xu,' describing the bird's action.
  • 声云取取 - Means 'its call was 'take, take,' describing the bird's sound.
  • 绪遂卒 - Means 'Wang Xu then died,' describing the outcome.
  • 出广异记 - Refers to the source of the information, likely a collection of strange and unusual stories.
  • 大歴八年 - Refers to the year 773 during the Tang Dynasty, a period of relative stability and cultural development.
  • 大鸟见武功 - Means 'a large bird was seen in Wugong,' describing the event.
  • 羣噪之 - Means 'it made a commotion,' describing the bird's sound.
  • 行营将张自芬射获之 - Means 'camp commander Zhang Zifen shot and caught it,' describing the action.
  • 肉翅狐首 - Means 'fleshy wings and fox head,' describing the bird's appearance.
  • 四足足有爪 - Means 'four feet with claws,' describing the bird's legs.
  • 广四尺 - Means 'four feet wide,' describing the size.
  • 出酉阳杂爼 - Refers to the source of the information, likely a historical or anecdotal collection.
  • 鹳鷒 - A type of bird, possibly a heron.
  • 一名堕羿 - Another name for the bird, possibly 'falling archer.'
  • 形似鹊 - Means 'looks like a magpie,' providing a comparison.
  • 人射之则衔矢反射人 - Means 'if shot, it would take the arrow and shoot it back,' describing the bird's behavior.
  • 出酉阳杂爼 - Refers to the source of the information, likely a historical or anecdotal collection.
  • 吐绶鸟 - A type of bird, possibly a parrot or another bird known for its colorful plumage.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 开元中 - The early years of the Kaiyuan era (713-741) during the Tang Dynasty, a period of prosperity and cultural flourishing.
  • 贝州 - Beizhou, a region in Hebei Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 天寳末 - The end of the Tianbao era (755-756) during the Tang Dynasty, a period marked by political turmoil and the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 大歴八年 - The year 773 during the Tang Dynasty, a period of relative stability and cultural development.
  • 武功 - Wugong, a place in Shaanxi Province, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 鹳鷒 - A type of bird, possibly a heron, which has cultural and mythological significance in Chinese literature.
  • 吐绶鸟 - A type of bird, possibly a parrot or another bird known for its colorful plumage, which has cultural and mythological significance in Chinese literature.

[5457]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 南山有鸟 - Refers to a bird living in the southern mountain, a common way to introduce a description of an animal.
  • 羽色多黒杂以黄白 - Describes the bird's feathers as mostly black with yellow and white mixed in.
  • 头颇似雉 - Indicates that the bird's head resembles that of a pheasant.
  • 有时吐物长数寸丹采彪炳 - Describes the bird's occasional behavior of spitting out something long and several inches, which is brightly colored and striking.
  • 形色类绶 - Suggests that the shape and color of the object resemble a ribbon or sash.
  • 因名为吐绶鸟 - Indicates that the bird is named for this unique behavior.
  • 避株鸟 - Another name for the bird, derived from its behavior of avoiding trees.
  • 杜鹃始阳相推而鸣 - Describes the cuckoo's behavior of starting to call in spring, often in a competitive or pushing manner.
  • 先鸣者吐血死 - Suggests that the first cuckoo to call will bleed to death, a superstitious belief.
  • 主离别 - Implies that hearing the cuckoo's call signifies separation or parting.
  • 不祥厌之 - Indicates that the cuckoo's call is considered inauspicious and should be avoided.
  • 当为犬声应之 - Suggests a method to counteract the bad luck by barking like a dog.
  • 蚊母鸟形如鹢嘴大而长 - Describes the mosquito bird as having a large, long beak like that of a bird called 鹈 (a type of water bird).
  • 池塘捕鱼而食 - Indicates that the mosquito bird catches and eats fish in ponds.
  • 每呌一声则有蚊蚋飞出其口 - Describes the bird's behavior of spitting out mosquitoes and gnats when it calls.
  • 俗云采其翎为扇可辟蚊子 - Suggests a folk belief that the feathers of the mosquito bird can be used to make fans that repel mosquitoes.
  • 桐花鸟 - Refers to a bird associated with the Chinese parasol tree (梧桐树).
  • 大如指五色毕具有冠似凤 - Describes the bird as being about the size of a finger, with all five colors and a crest resembling a phoenix.
  • 食桐花每桐结花即来桐花落即去 - Indicates that the bird eats the flowers of the Chinese parasol tree and appears when the tree blooms and disappears when the flowers fall.
  • 不知何之 - Suggests that the bird's destination after the flowers fall is unknown.
  • 真腊国大鸟 - Refers to a large bird in the kingdom of Chenla (真腊).
  • 浪鸟状似老鸱大如骆駞 - Describes the bird as resembling an old owl but as large as a camel.
  • 腾空而去 - Indicates that the bird can fly away quickly.
  • 百姓苦之 - Suggests that the people are troubled by the bird.
  • 真腊王取大牛肉中安小劒子两头尖利 - Describes a method used by the king of Chenla to kill the bird by placing a sharp knife in a piece of beef and letting the bird eat it.
  • 百舌 - Refers to a bird known for its ability to mimic various sounds.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鱼复县 - A county in ancient China, now part of Chongqing.
  • 酉阳杂爼 - A collection of miscellaneous records and anecdotes from the Western Han dynasty.
  • 岭表録异 - A book recording strange and unusual things from the southern regions of China.
  • 朝野佥载 - A collection of anecdotes and historical records from the Tang dynasty.
  • 真腊国 - An ancient kingdom in Southeast Asia, corresponding to modern-day Cambodia.
  • 葛浪山 - A mountain in the kingdom of Chenla (真腊).

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • Break down long, complex sentences into shorter, more readable segments while maintaining the logical flow of the original text.

Proper Nouns

  • 鱼复县 - A county in ancient China.
  • 酉阳杂爼 - A collection of miscellaneous records and anecdotes from the Western Han dynasty.
  • 岭表録异 - A book recording strange and unusual things from the southern regions of China.
  • 朝野佥载 - A collection of anecdotes and historical records from the Tang dynasty.
  • 真腊国 - An ancient kingdom in Southeast Asia, corresponding to modern-day Cambodia.
  • 葛浪山 - A mountain in the kingdom of Chenla (真腊).

[5458]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 百舌春啭夏至 - Describes the cuckoo's call in spring and summer, a common poetic image in Chinese literature.
  • 唯食蚯蚓 - Indicates that the cuckoo feeds solely on earthworms.
  • 正月后冻开蚓出而来 - Describes the phenomenon of earthworms emerging after the ice thaws in late winter or early spring.
  • 十月后蚓藏而往 - Describes the phenomenon of earthworms retreating into the ground in late autumn or early winter.
  • 葢物之相感也 - Means 'this is the mutual influence of things,' suggesting a natural or cosmic connection.
  • 江淮谓羣鹳旋飞为鹳井 - Refers to a local term in the Jianghuai region for a group of storks flying in a circular pattern, likened to a well.
  • 鹳亦好旋飞 - Indicates that storks also enjoy flying in circles.
  • 必有风雨 - Suggests that such behavior is often followed by rain or wind.
  • 人探巢取鹳子六十里旱能羣飞薄霄激雨雨为之散 - Describes a local belief that if people take stork chicks, the storks will fly high in the sky, causing rain to scatter.
  • 南方有鹳食虵 - Refers to a southern region where storks eat snakes.
  • 每遇巨石知其下有虵即于石前如道士禹歩其石阞然而转因得而噉 - Describes a ritual-like behavior where storks circle around large stones, sensing snakes beneath them, and then turn the stone to catch the snake.
  • 里人学其法者伺其养雏縁树以篾絙缚其巢鹳必作法而解之乃铺沙树底俾足迹所印而仿学之 - Describes how locals learn this method by observing storks' behavior and imitating their footprints in sand.
  • 大中末舒州奏众鸟成巢濶七尺髙一丈而燕雀鹰鹯水禽山鸟无不驯狎如一 - Describes a report from Shuzhou about a large nest built by various birds, including swallows, sparrows, eagles, and other birds, all coexisting peacefully.
  • 更有鸟人面緑毛嘴爪皆绀其声曰甘虫因谓之甘虫 - Refers to a bird with a human-like face, green feathers, and dark claws, whose voice is called 'Gan Chong.'
  • 时人画图鬻于坊市 - Describes how people drew pictures of this bird and sold them in the market.
  • 王蜀刑部侍郎李仁表寓居许州将入贡于春官时薛能尚书为镇先缮所业诗五十篇以为贽濡翰成轴于小亭凭几阅之未三五首有戴胜自檐飞入立于案几之上驯狎良久伸颈亸翼而舞向人若将语久之又转又舞如是者三超然飞去 - Describes a story about Li Renbiao, a magistrate from Wanshou, who was preparing to present his work to the Spring Official. While reading his poems, a hoopoe flew in and danced on the table, as if communicating with him.
  • 心异之不以告人翌日投诗薛大加礼待居数日以其女妻之 - Describes how Li Renbiao was amazed by the hoopoe's behavior and did not tell anyone. The next day, he presented his poems to Xue Neng, who treated him with great respect and later married his daughter to him.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 百舌 - Refers to the cuckoo, a bird often associated with spring in Chinese literature.
  • 江淮 - Refers to the Jianghuai region, an area in central China.
  • 舒州 - Refers to Shuzhou, a place in Anhui Province.
  • 大中末 - Refers to the late period of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 王蜀 - Refers to the state of Wanshou, a region in Sichuan during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 春官 - Refers to the Ministry of Rites, one of the six ministries in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 戴胜 - Refers to the hoopoe, a bird often associated with good fortune in Chinese culture.
  • 薛能 - Refers to Xue Neng, a prominent official during the Tang Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 百舌 - The cuckoo.
  • 江淮 - The Jianghuai region.
  • 舒州 - Shuzhou.
  • 大中末 - The late period of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 王蜀 - The state of Wanshou.
  • 春官 - The Ministry of Rites.
  • 戴胜 - The hoopoe.
  • 薛能 - Xue Neng.

[5459]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 北海大鸟 - Refers to a large bird in the Bohai Sea.
  • 其髙千里 - Describes the bird's height as a thousand miles, likely an exaggeration for emphasis.
  • 头文曰天 - The marking on its head is said to be 'Heaven'.
  • 胸文曰候 - The marking on its chest is said to be 'Observation' or 'Sign'.
  • 左翼文曰鹥 - The marking on its left wing is said to be 'Yi' (a type of bird).
  • 右翼文曰勒 - The marking on its right wing is said to be 'Le' (a type of bird or bird-like creature).
  • 举翼飞而其羽相切如雷风也 - When it flaps its wings, the feathers clash like thunder and wind.
  • 温璋为京兆尹 - Wen Zhang served as the Governor of Jingzhao (a region in ancient China).
  • 勇于杀戮 - Known for his bravery in killing.
  • 京邑惮之 - The people of the capital were afraid of him.
  • 闻挽铃而不见有人 - Heard the sound of a bell being pulled but saw no one.
  • 鵶 - Refers to a crow.
  • 探其鶵而来诉 - The crow was seeking help for its chicks.
  • 命吏随鵶所在而捕之 - Ordered officials to follow the crow to find the person taking its chicks.
  • 盘旋引吏至城外树间 - The crow circled and led the officials to a tree outside the city.
  • 果有人探其雏尚憇树下 - Indeed, there was a person taking the chicks, still resting under the tree.
  • 吏执送之府尹 - The officials arrested the person and brought them to the governor.
  • 毙捕雏者 - The governor ordered the person to be killed.
  • 王蜀永平二年 - The year Yongping 2 during the reign of Wang Shu (a historical figure).
  • 得北邙山章宏道所留瑞文于什邡之仙居山 - Found a divine text left by Zhang Hongdao on Mount Beimang, which was then moved to Xianju Mountain in Shifang.
  • 委汉州马歩使赵宏约缔搆观宇 - Entrusted the construction of a temple to Zhao Hongyue, the military governor of Han Prefecture.
  • 创天尊殿 - Built a hall dedicated to the Supreme Being.
  • 材石宏博 - The materials and stones used were extensive.
  • 功用甚多 - The project required a great deal of effort.
  • 架巨梁 - Constructing a large beam.
  • 工巧丁役三百余人 - More than three hundred skilled workers and laborers.
  • 缚拽鼓噪 - The workers were tying and pulling, creating a noisy scene.
  • 震动逺近 - The noise and activity shook the surrounding area.
  • 盘旋引吏至城外树间 - The crow circled and led the officials to a tree outside the city.
  • 栖于梁上 - The crow perched on the beam.
  • 随絙索上下 - The crow moved up and down the ropes.
  • 在众人中略无惊怖 - The crow showed no fear among the people.
  • 抚搦戏翫之 - The workers petted and played with the crow.
  • 梁既上毕鸟亦飞去 - Once the beam was in place, the crow flew away.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 北海 - Refers to the Bohai Sea, a significant body of water in northeastern China.
  • 温璋 - A historical figure who served as the Governor of Jingzhao.
  • 京兆尹 - The highest administrative official in the capital city.
  • 鵶 - A common bird, often associated with omens or messages in Chinese folklore.
  • 王蜀永平二年 - A specific year during the reign of Wang Shu, a historical figure.
  • 北邙山 - Mount Beimang, a famous mountain in Henan Province known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 什邡之仙居山 - Xianju Mountain in Shifang, a place known for its natural beauty and historical importance.
  • 天尊殿 - A hall dedicated to the Supreme Being, a common feature in Chinese temples.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 北海有大鸟其髙千里 - The sentence structure is elliptical, but it clearly describes a large bird in the Bohai Sea with a height of a thousand miles.
  • 闻挽铃而不见有人 - This phrase is elliptical and can be interpreted as the sound of a bell being pulled without seeing anyone.
  • 盘旋引吏至城外树间 - The crow's actions are described in a sequence that must be read as a single action leading to a specific location.
  • 缚拽鼓噪震动逺近 - This phrase describes the noisy and chaotic scene of the workers constructing the beam, which shook the surrounding area.
  • 栖于梁上随絙索上下在众人中略无惊怖 - The crow's behavior is described in a sequence that must be read as a continuous action, showing no fear among the people.

Proper Nouns

  • 北海 - The Bohai Sea.
  • 温璋 - A historical figure.
  • 京兆尹 - The highest administrative official in the capital city.
  • 鵶 - A crow.
  • 王蜀永平二年 - A specific year during the reign of Wang Shu.
  • 北邙山 - Mount Beimang.
  • 什邡之仙居山 - Xianju Mountain in Shifang.
  • 天尊殿 - A hall dedicated to the Supreme Being.
  • 赵宏约 - A historical figure who oversaw the construction of the temple.
  • 章宏道 - A historical figure who left a divine text on Mount Beimang.

[5460]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 顷年 - Refers to a recent past period, often used in historical narratives.
  • 黄鸎鶵 - Refers to a young yellow oriole bird.
  • 接翼 - Means 'to touch wings,' describing the close interaction between the birds.
  • 晓夜哀鸣 - Describes the birds' constant crying day and night.
  • 缭绕飞鸣 - Means 'flying and calling in a circling manner,' indicating the birds' distressed behavior.
  • 寸断 - Literally means 'cut into small pieces,' used here to describe the bird's intestines being torn apart by emotional distress.
  • 剖腹视之 - Means 'to cut open the belly and examine it,' a vivid description of the post-mortem examination.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太平广记 - A monumental work of Chinese literature compiled under the patronage of Emperor Taizong of the Song Dynasty, containing a vast array of stories and legends.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The official compilation of the Four Treasuries, a massive collection of Chinese literature and knowledge, commissioned by Emperor Qianlong of the Qing Dynasty.
  • 玉堂闲话 - A collection of anecdotes and tales, often used as a source for historical and literary references.
  • 东海 - Refers to the East China Sea, a significant body of water in East Asia.
  • 南海 - Refers to the South China Sea, another significant body of water in East Asia.
  • 吴 - Refers to the Wu region, an area in southeastern China known for its rich cultural and historical heritage.

Proper Nouns

  • 太平广记 - The title of the collection of stories and legends.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The title of the official compilation of the Four Treasuries.
  • 玉堂闲话 - The source of the story about the yellow orioles.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 既而风恶船破补治不能制 - Elliptical sentence meaning 'Soon after, the wind became fierce; the boat broke and could not be controlled even after repairs.'
  • 随风浪莫知所之 - Verb '之' is used as a verb of motion, meaning 'to go' or 'to move.'
  • 其侣懽然下石植缆登洲 - Verbs '下石' and '植缆' are idiomatic and require contextual interpretation.
  • 吸波吐浪去疾如风 - Metaphor '去疾如风' is a common Classical Chinese idiom meaning 'to move as fast as the wind.'

  • 刻石为鲸鱼 - Literal description of a monument or sculpture meaning 'Carved stone into a whale.'

  • 且命僧转经画像 - 且 means 'and,' 命 means 'to order,' 僧 means 'monk,' 转经 means 'to chant scriptures,' and 画像 means 'to make an image.' This phrase describes the father ordering a monk to chant scriptures and make an image.
  • 仅旬余 - 仅 means 'only' or 'just,' and 旬余 means 'ten days or so.' This phrase describes the duration of the events.

  • 夜与诸表昆季宿于学院 - 夜 means 'night,' 与 means 'with,' 诸 means 'various,' 表昆季 refers to cousins, and 宿于学院 means 'stayed in the school.' This phrase describes the official staying in the school with his cousins.
  • 来晨姑家方会客 - 来晨 means 'the next morning,' 姑家 means 'aunt's house,' 方 means 'just,' and 会客 means 'to meet guests.' This phrase describes the aunt's house hosting guests the next morning.
  • 夜梦十九人皆衣青緑罗拜 - 夜梦 means 'dreamed at night,' 十九人 means 'nineteen people,' 皆 means 'all,' 衣青緑 means 'wearing blue-green,' and 罗拜 means 'bowing in a row.' This phrase describes the official dreaming of nineteen people bowing in a row, all wearing blue-green.
  • 具告求生词防哀切 - 具告 means 'reported fully,' 求生词 means 'words of life,' and 防哀切 means 'deeply mournful.' This phrase describes the people in the dream reporting their situation and pleading for life with deep sorrow.
  • 某方闲居非有公府之事也 - 某 is a self-reference, 方 means 'just,' 闲居 means 'idle residence,' 非 means 'not,' 有 means 'have,' 公府 means 'official office,' and 之事 means 'matters.' This phrase describes the official stating that he is just idly residing and has no official duties.
  • 茍有阶縁固不惜奉救也 - 茍 means 'if,' 有 means 'have,' 阶縁 means 'opportunity,' 固 means 'certainly,' 不惜 means 'not hesitate,' and 奉救 means 'to offer help.' This phrase describes the official saying that if there is an opportunity, he will certainly not hesitate to offer help.

  • 既寤盥栉束带至堂省姑 - 既 means 'after,' 寤 means 'woke up,' 盥栉 means 'washed and combed,' 束带 means 'tied up his belt,' 至堂 means 'went to the hall,' and 省姑 means 'visited his aunt.' This phrase describes the official waking up, washing and combing, tying up his belt, and going to visit his aunt.
  • 见缶中有水而泛鼈焉 - 见 means 'saw,' 缶中 means 'in the jar,' 有水 means 'water,' and 泛鼈焉 means 'with a floating turtle.' This phrase describes the official seeing a jar containing water and a floating turtle.
  • 数之大小凡十九 - 数之 means 'counted them,' 大小 means 'sizes,' 凡 means 'total,' and 十九 means 'nineteen.' This phrase describes the official counting the turtles and finding there were nineteen in total.
  • 计其衣色亦略同也 - 计 means 'counted,' 其衣色 means 'their clothing color,' 亦 means 'also,' and 略同 means 'roughly the same.' This phrase describes the official noting that the colors of the clothing were roughly the same.
  • 遂告于姑具述所梦再拜请之 - 遂 means 'then,' 告于姑 means 'told his aunt,' 具述 means 'detailed,' 所梦 means 'the dream,' 再拜 means 'bowed twice,' and 请之 means 'asked for it.' This phrase describes the official telling his aunt about the dream in detail, bowing twice, and asking for it.
  • 姑亦不阻即命仆夫寘于器中躬诣水次放之 - 姑 means 'aunt,' 亦 means 'also,' 不阻 means 'did not object,' 即 means 'immediately,' 命仆夫 means 'ordered the servant,' 寘于器中 means 'put into a container,' 躬诣水次 means 'personally went to the water's edge,' and 放之 means 'released it.'   • 拥肿折查枿 - Describes overgrown or tangled branches and trees.

  • 士人乃呌何物怪魅敢凌人如此 - The scholar exclaims, 'What kind of monster dares to harm me like this?' '怪魅' refers to a supernatural creature or demon, and '凌人' means 'to harm or bully someone.'

  • 久之曰且解知悔叱令曳出不已在小门外 - After a while, the person says, 'Since you now understand and regret your actions, I will order you to be dragged out.' The scholar is then outside the small door. '曳出' means 'to drag out.'
  • 及归书堂已五更矣残灯犹在及明寻其踪迹东壁古堦下有小穴如栗守宫出入焉 - Upon returning to his study, it was already the fifth watch of the night, and the dim light of a candle remained. In the morning, he searched for the trail and found a small hole in the eastern wall, about the size of a chestnut, where a lizard (守宫) entered and exited. '五更' refers to the fifth watch of the night, and '守宫' is a type of lizard.
  • 士人即雇数夫发之深数丈有守宫十余石大者色赤长尺许盖其王也壤土如楼状士人聚苏焚之 - The scholar hired several laborers to dig deeper, and they found a mound of earth about the size of a small building. Inside, there were more than ten lizards, the largest of which was red and about a foot long, presumably the king. The scholar gathered the lizards and burned them. '守宫' refers to a type of lizard, and '聚苏焚之' means 'to gather and burn them.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '曝其皮于庭女行过其侧马皮蹶然而起卷女飞去' is elliptical and relies on context to clarify the sequence of events. A more complete version might be: 'The horse skin was exposed in the courtyard; as the woman passed by, the horse skin suddenly rose and rolled her up, causing her to fly away.'
  • The sentence '旬日皮复栖于桑树之上女化为蚕食桑叶吐丝成茧以衣被于人间' is also elliptical, with '旬日' (ten days) serving as a time marker. A more complete version might be: 'After ten days, the horse skin returned to rest on the mulberry tree, and the woman transformed into a silkworm, eating mulberry leaves, spinning silk, and creating cocoons to clothe the people of the world.'
  • The sentence '父母悔恨念之不已忽见蚕女乘流云驾此马侍卫数十人自天而下' is compact, with '忽见' (suddenly saw) marking a dramatic shift. A more complete version might be: 'The parents regretted and could not stop thinking about her; suddenly, they saw the silkworm woman riding on flowing clouds with this horse, accompanied by dozens of attendants, descending from the heavens.'
  • The sentence '太上以我孝能致身心不忘义授以九宫仙嫔之任长生于天矣无复忆念也' is elliptical, with '太上' (the Supreme Deity) as the subject. A more complete version might be: 'The Supreme Deity, because of my filial piety, allowed me to achieve spiritual transformation without forgetting righteousness, granting me the role of a celestial maid in the Nine Palaces, where I will live forever in heaven and will no longer be remembered.'

  • 广一丈深三丈 - Describes the dimensions of the well, with '广' meaning width and '深' meaning depth.
  • 酒美如肉 - The wine is as delicious as meat, emphasizing its richness.
  • 清澄如镜 - The wine is as clear and transparent as a mirror, highlighting its purity.
  • 上有玉樽玉 - The well has jade cups and ladles, suggesting a luxurious and sacred setting.
  • 取一樽复生焉 - If one takes a cup of wine, another one regenerates, indicating an inexhaustible source.
  • 与天同休无干时 - The wine is eternal and unaffected by time, suggesting immortality.
  • 石邉有脯焉 - There is also meat (dried meat) near the stone, adding to the description of the region's resources.
  • 味如麞脯 - The meat tastes like deer meat, emphasizing its quality.
  • 饮此酒人不生死此井 - Drinking this wine prevents death, as the well is a source of eternal life.
  • 问人与天同生 - The inhabitants of the well are as old as the heavens, suggesting they are immortal.
  • 虽男女不夫妇 - Despite being male and female, they do not marry, indicating a unique social structure.
  • 故言不生死 - Therefore, they do not die, reinforcing the idea of immortality.

  • 民国人长三寸 - The people of the  nation are only three inches tall, emphasizing their small size.
  • 日行千里而歩疾如飞 - They can travel a thousand miles in a day and move as fast as flying, indicating their incredible speed.
  • 每为海所吞 - They are often swallowed by sea creatures, suggesting a dangerous environment.
  • 其人亦有君子小人 - The people have both noble and common individuals, indicating a social hierarchy.
  • 如君子性能机巧 - Noble individuals are skilled and clever, emphasizing their intelligence.
  • 每为患 - They often cause trouble for the sea creatures, indicating a conflict.
  • 常刻土为己状 - They often carve clay figures of themselves, suggesting a form of self-representation.
  • 或数百聚于野水际 - Sometimes hundreds gather at the edge of the sea or water, indicating a communal activity.
  • 以为小人吞之而有患 - They use these figures to represent common people and face challenges, suggesting a symbolic or ritual practice.
  • 凡百千度后见真者过去亦不能食 - After many such attempts, even the real people cannot be eaten, indicating a protective mechanism.

  • 穿穴为国 - They dig holes to create their homes, indicating a subterranean lifestyle.
  • 或三十步五十步为一国 - Their homes are spaced thirty to fifty steps apart, indicating a structured community.
  • 如此不啻千万 - There are countless such communities, emphasizing the vastness of their population.
  • 春夏则食路草实 - In spring and summer, they eat the seeds of roadside grasses, indicating their diet.
  • 秋冬食草根 - In autumn and winter, they eat the roots of grasses, indicating seasonal changes in their diet.
  • 值暑则裸形 - In hot weather, they go naked, indicating their adaptation to the environment.
  • 遇寒则编细草为衣 - In cold weather, they weave fine grass into clothing, indicating their resourcefulness.
  • 亦解服气 - They also practice breathing exercises, indicating a focus on physical and spiritual well-being.
  • 鹄国 - Refers to a mythical land of birds, likely a fantastical or legendary place.

  • 为人自然有礼 - They are naturally polite, indicating a high level of social conduct.
  • 如经谕跪拜 - They perform bows and prostrations as if following a script, indicating a formal and ritualistic society.
  • 行千里百物不敢犯之 - They can travel a thousand miles without disturbing anything, indicating their peaceful nature.
  • 虽畏海 - Despite fearing sea creatures, they remain cautious, indicating a balance of fear and respect.
  • 陈章与齐桓公言 - Refers to a historical figure, Tanchang, and Duke Huan of Qi, suggesting a connection to historical events.
  • 此人鹄中不死而鹄亦一举千里 - These people do not die in the bird land and can also travel a thousand miles in a single flight, indicating their extraordinary abilities.

[5634]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 悉具一曰要食又击之饼饵羮炙罗于石上 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'all the food, including one day's supply, was prepared and placed on the stone.'
  • 良久饮食而久以金锥揷于石罅旁 - Means 'after eating for a long time, a golden cone was inserted into a crack in the stone.'
  • 旁防大喜取其锥而还所欲随击而办 - Means 'the guard was very happy to take the cone and return it, and whatever was desired could be obtained by striking the stone.'
  • 因是富侔国力常以珠玑赡其弟弟云 - Means 'because of this, he became as wealthy as a state, and he always used pearls and jade to support his brothers and sisters.'
  • 我或如兄得金锥也 - Means 'I, too, could get the golden cone like my brother.'
  • 旁防知其愚谕之不及乃如其言 - Means 'the guard knew he was foolish and tried to explain, but he did not understand, so he followed his words.'
  • 弟蚕之止得一金如常者 - Means 'his brother tried to raise silkworms and only got one gold piece, as usual.'
  • 谷种之复一茎植焉 - Means 'he tried to plant grains and only got one stalk growing.'
  • 将熟亦为鸟所啣 - Means 'when it was almost ripe, it was also taken by birds.'
  • 其弟大悦随之入山至鸟入处遇羣 - Means 'his brother was very happy and followed the birds into the mountains, where he encountered a group of ghosts.'
  • 见窃余锥者乃执之谓曰 - Means 'they saw someone who had stolen their remaining cone and seized him, saying...'
  • 尔欲为我筑糠三版乎尔欲鼻长一丈乎 - Means 'Do you want to build three layers of straw for me? Do you want your nose to grow a foot long?'
  • 其弟请筑糠三版三日饥困不成求哀于 - Means 'his brother asked to build three layers of straw, but after three days of hunger and exhaustion, he could not complete it and pleaded with the ghosts.'
  • 乃拔其鼻鼻如而归 - Means 'the ghosts then pulled out his nose, which was as long as a pig's, and he returned.'
  • 国人怪而聚观之慙恚而卒 - Means 'the people of the country were amazed and gathered to watch, and he was filled with shame and anger before dying.'
